directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: In which phase there was the highest absolute increase between the estimated and the actual cost?
explanation: The highest absolute difference between estimated & actual cost will be maximum for the phase for which manpower & difference between estimated & actual time is maximum among Design, Foundation & Construction. We can observe the manpower requirement in design is too low as compared to Foundation & construction, so it must not be the required answer. Manpower requirement is close between Foundation & Construction. However it is more for construction. The difference between estimated & actual time required for Foundation & Construction are 1 & 2 months. So, it’s clear that the absolute difference between estimated & actual cost must be more in Construction phase. Correct option is (c). We must avoid solving such questions by actual calculations.

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: For which phase of the project was the highest percentage increase in cost (as compared to the estimated cost)?
explanation: The percentage increase in actual cost as compared to estimated cost depends only on the percentage increase in estimated time as compared to the actual time. Percentage increase in estimated time as compared to actual time for Design = (5 – 3)/3 × 100% = 66% Percentage increase in estimated time as compared to actual time for Foundation = (3 – 2)/2 × 100% = 50% Percentage increase in estimated time as compared to actual time for Construction = (8 – 6)/6 × 100% = 33% So, the highest percentage increase in cost is for Design phase of the project. Correct option is (b)

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: What is the approximate value of the percentage overflow in costs during construction?
explanation: The percentage overflow in costs during construction is the percentage increase in the actual time in completion of construction phase as compared to estimated time for it. Percentage increase in estimated time as compared to actual time for Construction = (8 – 6)/6 × 100% = 33%. So, correct option is (b)

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: If internal finishing and painting can be started simultaneously at the end of construction, then what is the overflow of the project as a percentage of the estimated total time for completion of the project?
explanation: The estimated time for project is sum of estimated time for all 5 phases which is 3 + 2 + 6 + 3 + 2 = 16 Keys

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: At a production level of 500 TVs, the selling price per unit is such that there is a net profit of 20%. What will be the impact on the net profit if the selling price per unit is reduced by 16.66% and the total number of units produced is increased by 40%?

options.label: a
options.text: it will increase by less than 20% but more than 10%

options.label: b
options.text: it will increase by less than 30% but more than 20%

options.label: c
options.text: it will decrease by less than 15% but more than 5%

explanation: For n = 500, CP/unit = ₹20,000 SP/unit = ₹24,000 Profit/unit = ₹4000 Hence total profit = ₹20 lacs Now, if the production is increased by 40%, then the total number of units produced = 7000 units. CP/unit = ₹120000/7 SP/unit = ₹24000 Total profit = ₹20 lacs So, the net profit will be the same. Therefore, the correct answer is option (d)

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: For production levels of less than 1500 units, the variation of the average cost of production per unit has the following relationship with the volume of production:

options.label: c
options.text: decreases, then increases and then increases again

explanation: It can be calculated that the average cost decreases from 0 to 300 units, then increases for the the 301st unit, then decreases till the 800th unit and then increases till the 801st unit and then decreases again. Therefore, the correct answer is option (d)

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: The highest production cost per unit will be observed at the following level of production:
explanation: From the solution of Q 6. We can see that option (a) is the correct answer.

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: What should be the selling price per unit (in ₹) To achieve a break-even if the total number of units produced is 1000?
explanation: Total cost of production for 1000 units = 155 × 105. Hence, to obtain the break even, the SP/unit = ₹15,500. Therefore option (b) is the correct answer

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: What is the approximate additional cost for producing a TV after 920 units have been produced?
explanation: The additional cost of producing each item after 920 items were produced is only the variable cost as fixed cost is constant from 800 to 1500 items produced. The variable cost 800 to 1500 is represented by linear graph with x-axis show 800 to 1500 (items produced) & y-axis show amount from 70 lakh to 105 lakh So, the additional cost per item produced for this range (i.e. 800 to 1500) & so also for item produced after 920 were produced is the slope of this line showing the variable cost curve. Required additional cost = (105 lakh – 70 lakh)/(1500 – 800) = ₹35 lakh/700 = ₹5,000 Correct option is (b)

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: In which of the following years, is the total profit of Tata Tea the highest?
explanation: Expenditure for 1997 = 150 lakh + 150 lakh = 300 lakh Income for 1997 = 400 lakh + 600 lakh = 1000 lakh Profit for 1997 = Income – Expenditure = 1000 lakh - 300 lakh = ₹700 lakh Expenditure for 1999 = 150 lakh + 100 lakh = 250 lakh Income for 1999 = 600 lakh + 700 lakh = 1300 lakh Profit for 1997 = Income – Expenditure = 1300 lakh - 250 lakh = ₹1050 lakh Expenditure for 2002 = 200 lakh + 300 lakh = 500 lakh Income for 2002 = 500 lakh + 600 lakh = 1100 lakh Profit for 1997 = Income – Expenditure = 1100 lakh - 500 lakh = ₹600 lakh Expenditure for 1994 = 150 lakh + 250 lakh = 400 lakh Income for 1994 = 300 lakh + 500 lakh = 800 lakh Profit for 1997 = Income – Expenditure = 800 lakh - 400 lakh = ₹400 lakh The total profit of Tata Tea is highest for the year 1999 Correct option is (b)

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: For how many years during the period 1995 to 2003, for at least one of the two teas did the income and expenditure not follow the same trend, i.e., both of them did not decrease or did not increase together over the previous year ?
explanation: This can be done by observing all the 4 graphs. This happened for all the years leaving just 1995–96. So, it happened for 8 years. Correct option is (c)

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: In which of the following years was the percentage increase in the income of Bengal Tea over the previous year, the highest?
explanation: The percentage increase in income of Bengal Tea in 1995 over past year = (600 – 500)/500 × 100 % = 20% The percentage increase in income of Bengal Tea in 1997 over past year = (600 – 400)/400 × 100 % = 50% The percentage increase in income of Bengal Tea in 1999 over past year = (700 – 500)/500 × 100 % = 40% The percentage increase in income of Bengal Tea in 2002 over past year = (600 – 500)/500 × 100 % = 20% The percentage increase in income of Bengal Tea over the previous year is highest in 1997 Correct option is (b)

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: For how many years during from 1995 to 2003, was there a growth in income compared to that of the previous year but there is a decrease in the income from the year to the next year for each of the two teas?
explanation: This can be done by observing the graphs for Income for both the Teas. This happened for 1998–99 & 2001–02. So, correct option is (b)

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: In which of the following years during 1995 to 2003, did the profit of Assam Tea decrease by the maximum amount when compared to that of the previous year?
explanation: The profit of Assam tea for 1998 & 1999 is ₹300 lakh & ₹500 lakh So, it increased in 1999 The profit of Assam tea for 1999 & 2000 is ₹500 lakh & ₹250 lakh So, decrease in 2000 over 1999 = (250 – 500)/500 × 100% = –50% (–ve show decrease) The profit of Assam tea for 2000 & 2001 is ₹250 lakh & ₹50 lakh So, decrease in 2001 over 2000 = (50 – 250)/250 × 100% = –80% (–ve show decrease) The profit of Assam tea for 2002 & 2003 is ₹200 lakh & ₹200 lakh So, it didn’t changed in 2003 The decrease in profit of Assam tea over past year is maximum for 2001

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: How many of the following statements are correct? I. In terms of vote share, Labour and Liberal parties taken together lost 3.2 percent in 1998 but gained a total of 16 seats in the same period.   II. Democrats, Republicans and 35% of the Independents could have formed the government only in two election years. III. No party increased its vote share in every succeeding election. IV. In the 2000 elections, as compared to the 1996 elections, Republicans and Democrats taken together, gained more in terms of vote share than in terms of percentage of seats.
explanation: Statement I: Incorrect Vote share of Labour and Liberal party taken together in the year 1996 = 28.8 + 1.97 = 30.77 Vote share of Labour and Liberal party taken together in the year 1998 = 25.82 + 1.75 = 27.57 ∴ Loss of vote share = 3.2% Number of seats won by Labour and Liberal in the year 1996 = 143 Number of seats won by Labour and Liberal in the year 1998 = 158 ∴ Gain is 15 seats. Statement II: Correct Democrats, Republicans and 35% of the Independents could have formed the government in two elections 1998 and 2000. Statement III: Correct By observation, we find that no party increased its vote share in every succeeding election. Statement IV: Incorrect In 1996, vote share of the Republicans and Democrats = (20.29 + 6.12) = 26.41% In 2000, vote share of the Republicans and Democrats= (23.75 + 5.4) = 29.15% In 1996, percentage of seats of the Republicans and Democrats = (29.61 + 5.88) = 35.49% In 2000, percentage of seats of the Republicans and Democrats = (33.53 + 6.00) = 39.53%. Difference in the vote share = 2.74% Difference in the percentage of the seats = 4.04% So, two statements are correct (II & III).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: How many of the following statements are incorrect? I. In the year 2002, in terms of percentage of seats obtained, Democrats and Labour together lost the maximum. II. Liberals and Republicans taken together lost both in terms of vote share and the number of seats obtained in the year 2000. III. In the 2000 elections, all the parties lost in terms of number of seats. IV. The highest gain in the number of seats for Labour was in the year 2002.
explanation: Statement I: Incorrect Percentage of seats obtained by Democrats and Labour together in years 1996, 1998, 2000 and in 2002 were 55.49%, 59.29%, 54.45% and 52.09% respectively. In 2002, they lost only 2.36% but in 2000 elections, they lost 4.84%. Hence, (A) is incorrect. Statement II: Correct Vote share of the Liberals and Republicans together in the year 1998 = 6.91%. Vote share of the Liberals and Republicans together in the year 2000 = 6.88%. Number of seats obtained by the Liberals and Republicans together in the year 1998 = 44. Number of seats obtained by the Liberals and Republicans together in the year 2000 = 34. Statement III: Incorrect Difference in the number of seats won by Independents in 2000 and 1998 = +6 Difference in the number of seats won by Labour in 2000 and 1998 = –43 Difference in the number of seats won by Republican in 2000 and 1998 = –4 Difference in the number of seats won by Liberals in 2000 and 1998 = –6 Difference in the number of seats won by Democrats in 2000 and 1998 = –22 Statement IV: Correct Number of seats won by Labour party in the year 1996 = 132 Number of seats won by Labour party in the year 1998 = 148 Number of seats won by Labour party in the year 2000 = 105 Number of seats won by Labour party in the year 2002 = 165. So, 2 statements are incorrect (I & III)

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: How many of the following statements are correct? I. Democrats and Labour together improved their vote share by a larger margin in 1998 over 1996 as compared to the one in 2002 over 1996. II. The number of seats lost by the Democrats in the 2002 elections was less than the number of seats gained by the Republicans in the same year. III. In 2002, the Independents gained the most, both in terms of vote share and the number of seats obtained. IV. If 70% of the Independents had joined Labour, they could have formed a government in 2002 but not in 1996.
explanation: Statement I: Correct From graphs, we can conclude that the vote share of Democrats and Labour party in 1998 is greater than their combined vote share in 2002. Statement II: Correct Number of seats lost by Democrats in 2002 elections = (33.53 × 5.01 – 25.48 × 6.20) = 168 – 158 = 10 Number of seats gained by the Republicans in 2002 = (7.9 × 6.2 – 6 × 5.01) = 49 – 30 = 19. Statement III: Incorrect Independents gained the most in terms of vote share but in terms of the number of seats, labour gained the most over the 2000 election as they increased their seats from 105 in 2000 election to 165 in 2002 election. Statement IV: Incorrect. In the year 2002 as well as in the year 1996, 70% of Independents and Labour are eligible to form the Government as their share in the seats won is greater than 50% in each of the years. So, 2 statements are correct (I & II)

explanation: Statement 2005 2006 2007 I (Raymond Ltd share 2.78% 1.98% 1.96% in total sales of textile sector) II (Tata Steel share in 15.61% 14.89% 14.95% total sales of Iron & Steel sector) III (Glaxo Smithkline 12.16% 11.61% 11.19% Ltd Salaries & wages as % of sales IV (Raymond Ltd share 22.47% 8.83% 8.69% profit as % of sales) So, if we make graph for these values for I, II, III & IV we get the shape as shown in figure for I & IV only (after change of scale).
